View C++ Code (4965 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <set>
#include <cmath>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    int D, max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) {
        cin >> bounds[i];
    }

    int initial_length;
    cin >> initial_length;

    vector<int> head(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) {
        cin >> head[i];
    }

    vector<vector<int>> snake;
    snake.push_back(head);

    int F;
    cin >> F;
    vector<vector<int>> food(F);
    for (int i = 0; i < F; i++) {
        food[i].resize(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
            cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    cin >> O;
    set<vector<int>> obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; i++) {
        vector<int> obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
            cin >> obs[j];
        }
        obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    int current_length = initial_length;

    for (int turn = 0; turn < max_turns && !food.empty(); turn++) {
        // Find nearest food using Manhattan distance
        int target_idx = 0;
        double min_dist = 1e18;

        for (size_t i = 0; i < food.size(); i++) {
            double dist = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                dist += abs(head[j] - food[i][j]);
            }
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                target_idx = i;
            }
        }

        vector<int> target = food[target_idx];

        // Find best valid move (greedy: closest to target food)
        int best_axis = 0;
        int best_dir = 1;
        double best_dist = 1e18;
        bool found_move = false;

        for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
            for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                vector<int> new_head = head;
                new_head[axis] += dir;

                // Check bounds
                bool valid = true;
                for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                    if (new_head[j] < 0 || new_head[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!valid) continue;

                // Check obstacles
                if (obstacles.count(new_head)) continue;

                // Check self collision (all body except tail which moves away)
                bool collision = false;
                for (size_t i = 0; i < snake.size() - 1; i++) {
                    if (snake[i] == new_head) {
                        collision = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (collision) continue;

                found_move = true;

                // Distance to target food
                double dist = 0;
                for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                    dist += abs(new_head[j] - target[j]);
                }

                if (dist < best_dist) {
                    best_dist = dist;
                    best_axis = axis;
                    best_dir = dir;
                }
            }
        }

        if (!found_move) {
            // No move towards food found, try any valid move
            for (int axis = 0; axis < D && !found_move; axis++) {
                for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                    vector<int> new_head = head;
                    new_head[axis] += dir;

                    bool valid = true;
                    for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                        if (new_head[j] < 0 || new_head[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                            valid = false;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (!valid) continue;

                    if (obstacles.count(new_head)) continue;

                    bool collision = false;
                    for (size_t i = 0; i < snake.size() - 1; i++) {
                        if (snake[i] == new_head) {
                            collision = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (collision) continue;

                    best_axis = axis;
                    best_dir = dir;
                    found_move = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if (!found_move) {
            break;
        }

        // Execute move
        vector<int> new_head = head;
        new_head[best_axis] += best_dir;

        snake.insert(snake.begin(), new_head);
        head = new_head;

        // Check if food was eaten
        for (size_t i = 0; i < food.size(); i++) {
            if (food[i] == head) {
                food.erase(food.begin() + i);
                current_length++;
                break;
            }
        }

        // Remove tail if not growing
        if ((int)snake.size() > current_length) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        cout << best_axis << " " << best_dir << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    int D, max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) {
        cin >> bounds[i];

                        break;

                // Distance to target food

                }

                    for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                        if (new_head[j] < 0 || new_head[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                            valid = false;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (!valid) continue;

                    if (obstacles.count(new_head)) continue;

                    bool collision = false;
                    for (size_t i = 0; i < snake.size() - 1; i++) {
                        if (snake[i] == new_head) {
                            collision = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (collision) continue;

                    best_axis = axis;
                    best_dir = dir;
                    found_move = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if (!found_move) {
            break;
        }

        // Execute move
        vector<int> new_head = head;
        new_head[best_axis] += best_dir;

        snake.insert(snake.begin(), new_head);
        head = new_head;

        // Check if food was eaten
        for (size_t i = 0; i < food.size(); i++) {
            if (food[i] == head) {
                food.erase(food.begin() + i);
                current_length++;
                break;
            }
        }

        // Remove tail if not growing
        if ((int)snake.size() > current_length) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        cout << best_axis << " " << best_dir << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

 0.0000
[3D 25x25x25, 20 food, 100 obstacles] Food: 0/20, Turns: 0, End: Hit self, Time: 0.01s

N-Dimensional Snake - 3D 25x25x25, 20 food, 100 obstacles

View C++ Code (4965 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <set>
#include <cmath>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    int D, max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    vector<int> bounds(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) {
        cin >> bounds[i];
    }

    int initial_length;
    cin >> initial_length;

    vector<int> head(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) {
        cin >> head[i];
    }

    vector<vector<int>> snake;
    snake.push_back(head);

    int F;
    cin >> F;
    vector<vector<int>> food(F);
    for (int i = 0; i < F; i++) {
        food[i].resize(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
            cin >> food[i][j];
        }
    }

    int O;
    cin >> O;
    set<vector<int>> obstacles;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; i++) {
        vector<int> obs(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
            cin >> obs[j];
        }
        obstacles.insert(obs);
    }

    int current_length = initial_length;

    for (int turn = 0; turn < max_turns && !food.empty(); turn++) {
        // Find nearest food using Manhattan distance
        int target_idx = 0;
        double min_dist = 1e18;

        for (size_t i = 0; i < food.size(); i++) {
            double dist = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                dist += abs(head[j] - food[i][j]);
            }
            if (dist < min_dist) {
                min_dist = dist;
                target_idx = i;
            }
        }

        vector<int> target = food[target_idx];

        // Find best valid move (greedy: closest to target food)
        int best_axis = 0;
        int best_dir = 1;
        double best_dist = 1e18;
        bool found_move = false;

        for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
            for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                vector<int> new_head = head;
                new_head[axis] += dir;

                // Check bounds
                bool valid = true;
                for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                    if (new_head[j] < 0 || new_head[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!valid) continue;

                // Check obstacles
                if (obstacles.count(new_head)) continue;

                // Check self collision (all body except tail which moves away)
                bool collision = false;
                for (size_t i = 0; i < snake.size() - 1; i++) {
                    if (snake[i] == new_head) {
                        collision = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (collision) continue;

                found_move = true;

                // Distance to target food
                double dist = 0;
                for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                    dist += abs(new_head[j] - target[j]);
                }

                if (dist < best_dist) {
                    best_dist = dist;
                    best_axis = axis;
                    best_dir = dir;
                }
            }
        }

        if (!found_move) {
            // No move towards food found, try any valid move
            for (int axis = 0; axis < D && !found_move; axis++) {
                for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                    vector<int> new_head = head;
                    new_head[axis] += dir;

                    bool valid = true;
                    for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) {
                        if (new_head[j] < 0 || new_head[j] >= bounds[j]) {
                            valid = false;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (!valid) continue;

                    if (obstacles.count(new_head)) continue;

                    bool collision = false;
                    for (size_t i = 0; i < snake.size() - 1; i++) {
                        if (snake[i] == new_head) {
                            collision = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (collision) continue;

                    best_axis = axis;
                    best_dir = dir;
                    found_move = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if (!found_move) {
            break;
        }

        // Execute move
        vector<int> new_head = head;
        new_head[best_axis] += best_dir;

        snake.insert(snake.begin(), new_head);
        head = new_head;

        // Check if food was eaten
        for (size_t i = 0; i < food.size(); i++) {
            if (food[i] == head) {
                food.erase(food.begin() + i);
                current_length++;
                break;
            }
        }

        // Remove tail if not growing
        if ((int)snake.size() > current_length) {
            snake.pop_back();
        }

        cout << best_axis << " " << best_dir << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 22: Tetris Game (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to play Tetris optimally.

Your C# Tetris player will be tested with games ranging from short practice sessions to marathon games.

**Problem:**
Standard Tetris rules apply - variable width board (typically 10-20 columns),
pieces fall from top, clear lines by filling them completely, game over when pieces stack to the top.
The program receives pieces one at a time and must decide optimal placement.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
```
width height num_pieces
For each piece:
Line: piece_type (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L)
Program outputs: rotation column
Then receives: result (ok, cleared_N, gameover)
```

**Output format (stdout, 1 line per piece, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
rotation column
- rotation: 0-3 (number of 90° clockwise rotations)
- column: 0 to width-1 (leftmost column of piece)

**Piece Types:**
- I: 4x1 line piece
- O: 2x2 square piece
- T: T-shaped piece
- S: S-shaped piece (right-leaning)
- Z: Z-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- J: L-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- L: L-shaped piece (right-leaning)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable board widths and long game sequences
4. Must place a peice within 5 seconds of it being written to STDIN.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on game complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Write an AI that plays Tetris. Given the current board state and the next piece, choose where to place it to survive as long as possible and clear as many lines as you can. Good play means keeping the board flat, avoiding buried holes, and setting up multi-line clears.

Subpasses increase the board size and speed, demanding smarter lookahead and evaluation. The AI must balance short-term survival with long-term board health.

View C# Code (5605 chars)
using System;
using System.Linq;

class TetrisAI
{
    // Piece rotations defined as arrays of (col, row) offsets
    static readonly int[][][] PIECES = {
        // I - 4x1 line piece
        new[] { new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0, 3, 0 }, new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0, 3 } },
        // O - 2x2 square piece
        new[] { new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 } },
        // T - T-shaped piece
        new[] {
            new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0, 1, 1 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 0, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2 }
        },
        // S - S-shaped piece
        new[] { new[] { 1, 0, 2, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 }, new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2 } },
        // Z - Z-shaped piece
        new[] { new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 2, 1 }, new[] { 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 0, 2 } },
        // J - J-shaped piece (left-leaning L)
        new[] {
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 1, 0, 2, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 0 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 1, 2 }
        },
        // L - L-shaped piece (right-leaning L)
        new[] {
            new[] { 2, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 1, 2 }
        }
    };

    int width, height;
    int[] col_heights;

    static void Main()
    {
        new TetrisAI().Run();
    }

    void Run()
    {
        // Parse game parameters
        string[] input = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        width = int.Parse(input[0]);
        height = int.Parse(input[1]);
        int num_pieces = int.Parse(input[2]);

        col_heights = new int[width];

        // Process each piece
        for (int i = 0; i < num_pieces; i++)
        {
            char piece_type = Console.ReadLine().Trim()[0];
            int piece_idx = "IOTSZJL".IndexOf(piece_type);

            // Find and output best placement
            var best = FindBestPlacement(piece_idx);
            Console.WriteLine($"{best.rotation} {best.column}");
            Console.Out.Flush();

            // Read game result
            string result = Console.ReadLine();
            if (result == "gameover")
                break;

            // Update board state with placement
            PlacePiece(piece_idx, best.rotation, best.column);

            // Handle line clears
            if (result.StartsWith("cleared_"))
            {
                int cleared_lines = int.Parse(result.Substring(8));
                for (int j = 0; j < width; j++)
                    col_heights[j] = Math.Max(0, col_heights[j] - cleared_lines);
            }
        }
    }

    (int rotation, int column) FindBestPlacement(int piece_idx)
    {
        int best_rotation = 0, best_column = 0;
        int best_height = int.MaxValue;

        int[][] rotations = PIECES[piece_idx];

        for (int rot = 0; rot < rotations.Length; rot++)
        {
            int[] shape = rotations[rot];

            // Calculate piece bounding box
            int min_col = int.MaxValue, max_col = int.MinValue, max_row = int.MinValue;
            for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
            {
                min_col = Math.Min(min_col, shape[i]);
                max_col = Math.Max(max_col, shape[i]);
                max_row = Math.Max(max_row, shape[i + 1]);
            }

            int piece_width = max_col - min_col + 1;

            // Try each valid column position
            for (int col = 0; col <= width - piece_width; col++)
            {
                int landing_height = 0;

                // Calculate where piece lands for this position
                for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
                {
                    int actual_col = col + shape[i] - min_col;
                    int cell_row = shape[i + 1];
                    int final_height = col_heights[actual_col] + max_row - cell_row + 1;
                    landing_height = Math.Max(landing_height, final_height);
                }

                // Check if placement is valid and better than current best
                if (landing_height <= height && landing_height < best_height)
                {
                    best_height = landing_height;
                    best_rotation = rot;
                    best_column = col;
                }
            }
        }

        return (best_rotation, best_column);
    }

    void PlacePiece(int piece_idx, int rotation, int column)
    {
        int[] shape = PIECES[piece_idx][rotation];

        // Calculate bounding box
        int min_col = int.MaxValue, max_row = int.MinValue;
        for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
        {
            min_col = Math.Min(min_col, shape[i]);
            max_row = Math.Max(max_row, shape[i + 1]);
        }

        // Find landing height
        int landing_height = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
        {
            int actual_col = column + shape[i] - min_col;
            int cell_row = shape[i + 1];
            int final_height = col_heights[actual_col] + max_row - cell_row + 1;
            landing_height = Math.Max(landing_height, final_height);
        }

        // Update column heights
        for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
        {
            int actual_col = column + shape[i] - min_col;
            int cell_row = shape[i + 1];
            int block_height = landing_height - max_row + cell_row;
            col_heights[actual_col] = Math.Max(col_heights[actual_col], block_height);
        }
    }
}

View C# Code (5605 chars)
using System;
using System.Linq;

class TetrisAI
{
    // Piece rotations defined as arrays of (col, row) offsets
    static readonly int[][][] PIECES = {
        // I - 4x1 line piece
        new[] { new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0, 3, 0 }, new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0, 3 } },
        // O - 2x2 square piece
        new[] { new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 } },
        // T - T-shaped piece
        new[] {
            new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 0, 1, 1 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 0, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2 }
        },
        // S - S-shaped piece
        new[] { new[] { 1, 0, 2, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 }, new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2 } },
        // Z - Z-shaped piece
        new[] { new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 2, 1 }, new[] { 1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 0, 2 } },
        // J - J-shaped piece (left-leaning L)
        new[] {
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 1, 0, 2, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 0 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 1, 2 }
        },
        // L - L-shaped piece (right-leaning L)
        new[] {
            new[] { 2, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 2, 1, 2 }
        }
    };

    int width, height;
    int[] col_heights;

    static void Main()
    {
        new TetrisAI().Run();
    }

    void Run()
    {
        // Parse game parameters
        string[] input = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        width = int.Parse(input[0]);
        height = int.Parse(input[1]);
        int num_pieces = int.Parse(input[2]);

        col_heights = new int[width];

        // Process each piece
        for (int i = 0; i < num_pieces; i++)
        {
            char piece_type = Console.ReadLine().Trim()[0];
            int piece_idx = "IOTSZJL".IndexOf(piece_type);

            // Find and output best placement
            var best = FindBestPlacement(piece_idx);
            Console.WriteLine($"{best.rotation} {best.column}");
            Console.Out.Flush();

            // Read game result
            string result = Console.ReadLine();
            if (result == "gameover")
                break;

            // Update board state with placement
            PlacePiece(piece_idx, best.rotation, best.column);

            // Handle line clears
            if (result.StartsWith("cleared_"))
            {
                int cleared_lines = int.Parse(result.Substring(8));
                for (int j = 0; j < width; j++)
                    col_heights[j] = Math.Max(0, col_heights[j] - cleared_lines);
            }
        }
    }

    (int rotation, int column) FindBestPlacement(int piece_idx)
    {
        int best_rotation = 0, best_column = 0;
        int best_height = int.MaxValue;

        int[][] rotations = PIECES[piece_idx];

        for (int rot = 0; rot < rotations.Length; rot++)
        {
            int[] shape = rotations[rot];

            // Calculate piece bounding box
            int min_col = int.MaxValue, max_col = int.MinValue, max_row = int.MinValue;
            for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
            {
                min_col = Math.Min(min_col, shape[i]);
                max_col = Math.Max(max_col, shape[i]);
                max_row = Math.Max(max_row, shape[i + 1]);
            }

            int piece_width = max_col - min_col + 1;

            // Try each valid column position
            for (int col = 0; col <= width - piece_width; col++)
            {
                int landing_height = 0;

                // Calculate where piece lands for this position
                for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
                {
                    int actual_col = col + shape[i] - min_col;
                    int cell_row = shape[i + 1];
                    int final_height = col_heights[actual_col] + max_row - cell_row + 1;
                    landing_height = Math.Max(landing_height, final_height);
                }

                // Check if placement is valid and better than current best
                if (landing_height <= height && landing_height < best_height)
                {
                    best_height = landing_height;
                    best_rotation = rot;
                    best_column = col;
                }
            }
        }

        return (best_rotation, best_column);
    }

    void PlacePiece(int piece_idx, int rotation, int column)
    {
        int[] shape = PIECES[piece_idx][rotation];

        // Calculate bounding box
        int min_col = int.MaxValue, max_row = int.MinValue;
        for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
        {
            min_col = Math.Min(min_col, shape[i]);
            max_row = Math.Max(max_row, shape[i + 1]);
        }

        // Find landing height
        int landing_height = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
        {
            int actual_col = column + shape[i] - min_col;
            int cell_row = shape[i + 1];
            int final_height = col_heights[actual_col] + max_row - cell_row + 1;
            landing_height = Math.Max(landing_height, final_height);
        }

        // Update column heights
        for (int i = 0; i < shape.Length; i += 2)
        {
            int actual_col = column + shape[i] - min_col;
            int cell_row = shape[i + 1];
            int block_height = landing_height - max_row + cell_row;
            col_heights[actual_col] = Math.Max(col_heights[actual_col], block_height);
        }
    }
}

Test 23: Lunar Lander Game Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to play Lunar Lander in real-time.

Your Rust lunar lander controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from simple
training environments to very complex landing sites.

Control a lunar lander to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a 2D voxel map and continuous state updates, and must output thrust/turn
commands to land safely on the target.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time. If your code
doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the io buffer fills up, the ship will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes that
async code doesn't have. Multithreaded / asyncio code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Physics:**
- Gravity pulls lander down constantly
- Thrust applies force in lander's facing direction
- Fuel is limited
- Must land softly on target (low velocity)

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal numbers):**
Initial map:
```
width height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y target_x target_y
map rows (. = empty, # = obstacle, S = start, T = target, L = landing zone)
```

Then you recieve continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds):
```
x y vx vy angle angular_vel fuel_remaining
```
x and y are in meters, from the worlds top left, and y increases downward.
vx and vy are in m/s.
angle is in radians, 0 is facing up.
angular_vel is in radians per second. + is clockwise, - is counterclockwise.
fuel_remaining is seconds at max_thrust.

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following instructions at a frequency of at least 20hz:
```
thrust turn
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max thrust)
- turn: -1.0 to 1.0 (turning rate, negative = left, positive = right)

To be crystal clear: that's two decimal numbers seperated by a space, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (so it misses 2 consecutive updates), the engine
will automatically cut power. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

Map size can vary from 100x50 to multi-gb maps, so be efficient with memory and processing.

Thrust and gravity are in m/s/s. 100% engine burn burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

Lander size is approximatly 1x1m, and the 2D map grid size is 1m x 1m.

Turn is in radians per second. The lander can not rotate fully around and will clamp rotation to
facing down if attempted.

Y increases downward. Positive vy means moving downward. Gravity is +g.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 2m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries
- Complete landing within time limit

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into obstacle or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function. Assume only the standard library is available.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft softly on the Moon. The lander starts above the surface with some velocity, and the AI must control the main thruster and side jets to touch down gently — too fast and it crashes, too much fuel wasted and it runs dry.

Subpasses vary the starting altitude, velocity, and terrain. The AI must handle 2D physics, fuel management, and real-time control to achieve a safe landing.

 0.0000
[100x50 - simple landing] End: crashed, Distance: 42.2m, Time: 3.76s

Lunar Lander - 100x50 - simple landing

View Rust Code (4675 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufReader};
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::thread;
use std::time::Duration;
use std::f64::consts::PI;

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct State {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    vx: f64,
    vy: f64,
    angle: f64,
    angular_vel: f64,
    fuel: f64,
}

fn main() {
    // Read header and map in initial scope so locks are released
    let (gravity, max_thrust, target_x, target_y) = {
        let stdin = io::stdin();
        let mut reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());
        let mut line = String::new();

        // Read header
        reader.read_line(&mut line).expect("Failed to read header");
        let header: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace()
            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
            .collect();
        if header.len() < 5 {
            panic!("Invalid header");
        }
        let gravity = header[2];
        let max_thrust = header[3];

        // Read coords
        line.clear();
        reader.read_line(&mut line).expect("Failed to read coords");
        let coords: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace()
            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
            .collect();
        if coords.len() < 4 {
            panic!("Invalid coords");
        }
        let target_x = coords[2];
        let target_y = coords[3];

        // Read and skip map
        let height = header[1] as usize;
        for _ in 0..height {
            line.clear();
            reader.read_line(&mut line).expect("Failed to read map");
        }

        (gravity, max_thrust, target_x, target_y)
    }; // Lock is released here

    let state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None::<State>));
    let state_clone = state.clone();

    // Spawn reader thread to continuously update state
    thread::spawn(move || {
        let stdin = io::stdin();
        let reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());

        for line in reader.lines() {
            if let Ok(line) = line {
                let parts: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace()
                    .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
                    .collect();

                if parts.len() >= 7 {
                    let new_state = State {
                        x: parts[0],
                        y: parts[1],
                        vx: parts[2],
                        vy: parts[3],
                        angle: parts[4],
                        angular_vel: parts[5],
                        fuel: parts[6],
                    };

                    if let Ok(mut state_lock) = state_clone.lock() {
                        *state_lock = Some(new_state);
                    }
                }
            } else {
                break;
            }
        }
    });

    // Main control loop - output at 20Hz (50ms per iteration)
    loop {
        if let Ok(state_lock) = state.lock() {
            if let Some(current_state) = *state_lock {
                let (thrust, turn) = calculate_command(
                    current_state,
                    target_x,
                    target_y,
                    gravity,
                    max_thrust,
                );

                println!("{:.2} {:.2}", thrust, turn);
                let _ = io::stdout().flush();
            }
        }

        thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
    }
}

fn calculate_command(
    state: State,
    target_x: f64,
    target_y: f64,
    _gravity: f64,
    _max_thrust: f64,
) -> (f64, f64) {
    let dx = target_x - state.x;
    let dy = target_y - state.y;
    let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
    let speed = (state.vx * state.vx + state.vy * state.vy).sqrt();

    // Thrust control based on descent rate
    let mut thrust = 0.5;

    if state.vy > 5.0 {
        // Falling very fast - full thrust
        thrust = 1.0;
    } else if state.vy > 3.0 {
        thrust = 0.8;
    } else if state.vy > 1.5 {
        thrust = 0.6;
    } else if state.vy > 0.5 {
        thrust = 0.4;
    } else if state.vy > 0.0 {
        thrust = 0.2;
    } else if speed < 2.0 && dist < 10.0 {
        // Near target with low speed - minimal thrust to avoid bouncing
        thrust = 0.1;
    } else {
        thrust = 0.3;
    }

    // Turn control to face target
    // angle = 0 is up, angle = π/2 is right, angle = π is down
    // In screen coords where y increases downward, target angle is:
    let desired_angle = dx.atan2(-dy);
    let angle_diff = normalize_angle(desired_angle - state.angle);
    let turn = (angle_diff * 0.8).clamp(-1.0, 1.0);

    (thrust, turn)
}

fn normalize_angle(mut angle: f64) -> f64 {
    while angle > PI {
        angle -= 2.0 * PI;
    }
    while angle < -PI {
        angle += 2.0 * PI;
    }
    angle
}

View Rust Code (4675 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufReader};
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::thread;
use std::time::Duration;
use std::f64::consts::PI;

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct State {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    vx: f64,
    vy: f64,
    angle: f64,
    angular_vel: f64,
    fuel: f64,
}

fn main() {
    // Read header and map in initial scope so locks are released
    let (gravity, max_thrust, target_x, target_y) = {
        let stdin = io::stdin();
        let mut reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());
        let mut line = String::new();

        // Read header
        reader.read_line(&mut line).expect("Failed to read header");
        let header: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace()
            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
            .collect();
        if header.len() < 5 {
            panic!("Invalid header");
        }
        let gravity = header[2];
        let max_thrust = header[3];

        // Read coords
        line.clear();
        reader.read_line(&mut line).expect("Failed to read coords");
        let coords: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace()
            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
            .collect();
        if coords.len() < 4 {
            panic!("Invalid coords");
        }
        let target_x = coords[2];
        let target_y = coords[3];

        // Read and skip map
        let height = header[1] as usize;
        for _ in 0..height {
            line.clear();
            reader.read_line(&mut line).expect("Failed to read map");
        }

        (gravity, max_thrust, target_x, target_y)
    }; // Lock is released here

    let state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None::<State>));
    let state_clone = state.clone();

    // Spawn reader thread to continuously update state
    thread::spawn(move || {
        let stdin = io::stdin();
        let reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());

        for line in reader.lines() {
            if let Ok(line) = line {
                let parts: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace()
                    .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
                    .collect();

                if parts.len() >= 7 {
                    let new_state = State {
                        x: parts[0],
                        y: parts[1],
                        vx: parts[2],
                        vy: parts[3],
                        angle: parts[4],
                        angular_vel: parts[5],
                        fuel: parts[6],
                    };

                    if let Ok(mut state_lock) = state_clone.lock() {
                        *state_lock = Some(new_state);
                    }
                }
            } else {
                break;
            }
        }
    });

    // Main control loop - output at 20Hz (50ms per iteration)
    loop {
        if let Ok(state_lock) = state.lock() {
            if let Some(current_state) = *state_lock {
                let (thrust, turn) = calculate_command(
                    current_state,
                    target_x,
                    target_y,
                    gravity,
                    max_thrust,
                );

                println!("{:.2} {:.2}", thrust, turn);
                let _ = io::stdout().flush();
            }
        }

        thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
    }
}

fn calculate_command(
    state: State,
    target_x: f64,
    target_y: f64,
    _gravity: f64,
    _max_thrust: f64,
) -> (f64, f64) {
    let dx = target_x - state.x;
    let dy = target_y - state.y;
    let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
    let speed = (state.vx * state.vx + state.vy * state.vy).sqrt();

    // Thrust control based on descent rate
    let mut thrust = 0.5;

    if state.vy > 5.0 {
        // Falling very fast - full thrust
        thrust = 1.0;
    } else if state.vy > 3.0 {
        thrust = 0.8;
    } else if state.vy > 1.5 {
        thrust = 0.6;
    } else if state.vy > 0.5 {
        thrust = 0.4;
    } else if state.vy > 0.0 {
        thrust = 0.2;
    } else if speed < 2.0 && dist < 10.0 {
        // Near target with low speed - minimal thrust to avoid bouncing
        thrust = 0.1;
    } else {
        thrust = 0.3;
    }

    // Turn control to face target
    // angle = 0 is up, angle = π/2 is right, angle = π is down
    // In screen coords where y increases downward, target angle is:
    let desired_angle = dx.atan2(-dy);
    let angle_diff = normalize_angle(desired_angle - state.angle);
    let turn = (angle_diff * 0.8).clamp(-1.0, 1.0);

    (thrust, turn)
}

fn normalize_angle(mut angle: f64) -> f64 {
    while angle > PI {
        angle -= 2.0 * PI;
    }
    while angle < -PI {
        angle += 2.0 * PI;
    }
    angle
}

Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

 0.0000
[100x100x50 - simple 3D landing] End: Compilation error: 
Compilation failed:
tmpwthzg_lk.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpwthzg_lk.cpp(184): error C2065: 'M_PI': undeclared identifier
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpwthzg_lk.cpp(184): error C2065: 'M_PI': undeclared identifier
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpwthzg_lk.cpp(185): error C2065: 'M_PI': undeclared identifier
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpwthzg_lk.cpp(185): error C2065: 'M_PI': undeclared identifier
, Distance: infm, Time: 0.00s

3D Lunar Lander - 100x100x50 - simple 3D landing

View C++ Code (7747 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <cmath>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <fstream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <cstring>

using namespace std;

// State structure matching input format
struct State {
    float x, y, z;
    float vx, vy, vz;
    float pitch, yaw, roll;
    float pitch_rate, yaw_rate, roll_rate;
    float fuel_remaining;
};

struct TerrainCell {
    uint16_t ground_top;
    uint16_t floating_bottom;
    uint16_t floating_top;
};

// Global configuration
int terrain_width, terrain_depth, terrain_height;
float gravity, max_thrust;
float target_x, target_y, target_z;
vector<vector<TerrainCell>> terrain;

// Threading and synchronization
State current_state = {};
mutex state_mutex;
atomic<bool> terrain_loaded(false);
atomic<bool> running(true);
queue<State> state_queue;
mutex queue_mutex;

// Read little-endian uint16
uint16_t read_u16_le(FILE* f) {
    unsigned char lo = fgetc(f);
    unsigned char hi = fgetc(f);
    return ((uint16_t)hi << 8) | (uint16_t)lo;
}

// Load terrain from binary file
void load_terrain(const string& filename) {
    FILE* f = fopen(filename.c_str(), "rb");
    if (!f) return;

    terrain.assign(terrain_width, vector<TerrainCell>(terrain_depth));

    for (int x = 0; x < terrain_width; x++) {
        for (int y = 0; y < terrain_depth; y++) {
            uint16_t a = read_u16_le(f);
            uint16_t b = read_u16_le(f);
            uint16_t c = read_u16_le(f);
            terrain[x][y] = {a, b, c};
        }
    }

    fclose(f);
    terrain_loaded = true;
}

// Check if position is solid terrain
bool is_solid(float px, float py, float pz) {
    // Out of bounds check
    if (px < 0 || px >= terrain_width || py < 0 || py >= terrain_depth ||
        pz < 0 || pz >= terrain_height) {
        return true;
    }

    if (!terrain_loaded) return false;

    int ix = (int)floor(px);
    int iy = (int)floor(py);
    int iz = (int)floor(pz);

    if (ix < 0 || ix >= terrain_width || iy < 0 || iy >= terrain_depth)
        return true;

    const TerrainCell& cell = terrain[ix][iy];

    // Check ground rock
    if (iz < cell.ground_top) return true;
    // Check floating rock
    if (iz >= cell.floating_bottom && iz < cell.floating_top) return true;

    return false;
}

// Thread: Read stdin and populate state queue
void stdin_reader() {
    string line;

    // Read header line 1: width depth height gravity max_thrust
    cin >> terrain_width >> terrain_depth >> terrain_height >> gravity >> max_thrust;

    // Read header line 2: start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
    float start_x, start_y, start_z;
    cin >> start_x >> start_y >> start_z;
    cin >> target_x >> target_y >> target_z;

    // Read header line 3: terrain file path
    cin.ignore(1);
    getline(cin, line);
    string terrain_path = line;

    // Start terrain loading in background thread
    thread terrain_loader(load_terrain, terrain_path);
    terrain_loader.detach();

    // Read STATE marker
    string marker;
    cin >> marker;

    // Read state updates continuously
    while (running) {
        State s;
        if (!(cin >> s.x >> s.y >> s.z >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                  >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                  >> s.pitch_rate >> s.yaw_rate >> s.roll_rate
                  >> s.fuel_remaining)) {
            break;
        }

        {
            lock_guard<mutex> lock(queue_mutex);
            state_queue.push(s);
        }
    }

    running = false;
}

// Thread: Control algorithm
void controller() {
    auto last_output_time = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();

    while (running) {
        // Get latest state from queue
        State s;
        {
            lock_guard<mutex> lock(queue_mutex);
            if (state_queue.empty()) {
                this_thread::sleep_for(chrono::milliseconds(2));
                continue;
            }
            s = state_queue.front();
            state_queue.pop();
        }

        // Update global state
        {
            lock_guard<mutex> lock(state_mutex);
            current_state = s;
        }

        // Calculate relative position to target
        float dx = target_x - s.x;
        float dy = target_y - s.y;
        float dz = target_z - s.z;
        float horizontal_dist = sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);

        float thrust = 0.5f;
        float pitch_cmd = 0.0f;
        float yaw_cmd = 0.0f;
        float roll_cmd = 0.0f;

        // Phase 1: High altitude descent (dz > 30m or still falling fast)
        if (dz > 30.0f || s.vz < -5.0f) {
            // Maneuver horizontally toward target
            if (horizontal_dist > 5.0f) {
                float target_heading = atan2(dy, dx);
                float heading_error = target_heading - s.yaw;

                // Normalize heading error to [-pi, pi]
                while (heading_error > M_PI) heading_error -= 2 * M_PI;
                while (heading_error < -M_PI) heading_error += 2 * M_PI;

                yaw_cmd = heading_error * 0.3f;
                yaw_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, yaw_cmd));
            }

            // Control descent rate: aim for about -2 m/s
            if (s.vz > -1.5f) {
                thrust = 0.4f;  // Let gravity pull down
            } else if (s.vz < -3.0f) {
                thrust = 0.65f;  // Slow down descent
            } else {
                thrust = 0.5f;
            }
        }
        // Phase 2: Medium altitude stabilization
        else if (dz > 10.0f) {
            // Fine horizontal approach
            pitch_cmd = dx * 0.03f;
            yaw_cmd = -dy * 0.03f;

            pitch_cmd = max(-0.5f, min(0.5f, pitch_cmd));
            yaw_cmd = max(-0.5f, min(0.5f, yaw_cmd));

            // Gentle descent
            if (s.vz > -1.0f) {
                thrust = 0.5f;
            } else if (s.vz < -2.0f) {
                thrust = 0.6f;
            } else {
                thrust = 0.55f;
            }
        }
        // Phase 3: Final approach (landing)
        else {
            // Counteract horizontal velocity
            pitch_cmd = s.vx * 0.12f;
            yaw_cmd = -s.vy * 0.08f;

            // Very gentle descent - minimum thrust variation
            if (abs(s.vz) < 0.2f) {
                thrust = 0.5f;
            } else if (s.vz > -0.3f) {
                thrust = 0.48f;
            } else {
                thrust = 0.52f;
            }
        }

        // Clamp all commands to valid ranges
        thrust = max(0.0f, min(1.0f, thrust));
        pitch_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, pitch_cmd));
        yaw_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, yaw_cmd));
        roll_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, roll_cmd));

        // Output control command with proper precision
        cout << fixed << setprecision(4)
             << thrust << " " << pitch_cmd << " " << yaw_cmd << " " << roll_cmd << "\n";
        cout.flush();

        // Rate limiting to maintain ~10Hz output
        auto now = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();
        auto ms_elapsed = chrono::duration_cast<chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time).count();
        if (ms_elapsed < 100) {
            this_thread::sleep_for(chrono::milliseconds(100 - ms_elapsed));
        }
        last_output_time = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();
    }
}

int main() {
    // Disable synchronization between C and C++ stdio for faster I/O
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    // Start reader thread (handles stdin)
    thread reader_thread(stdin_reader);

    // Start controller thread (handles control algorithm)
    thread control_thread(controller);

    // Wait for both threads to complete
    reader_thread.join();
    control_thread.join();

    return 0;
}

// Thread: Read stdin and populate state queue
void stdin_reader() {
    string line;

    // Read header line 1: width depth height gravity max_thrust
    cin >> terrain_width >> terrain_depth >> terrain_height >> gravity >> max_thrust;

    // Read header line 2: start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z

                  >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll

                  >> s.fuel_remaining)) {

}

// Thread: Control algorithm
void controller() {
    auto last_output_time = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();

    while (running) {
        // Get latest state from queue

        {
            lock_guard<mutex> lock(queue_mutex);

            }
            s = state_queue.front();

            lock_guard<mutex> lock(state_mutex);
            current_state = s;
        }

        // Calculate relative position to target

        float dy = target_y - s.y;
        float dz = target_z - s.z;
        float horizontal_dist = sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);

        float thrust = 0.5f;
        float pitch_cmd = 0.0f;
        float yaw_cmd = 0.0f;

                float target_heading = atan2(dy, dx);
                float heading_error = target_heading - s.yaw;

                // Normalize heading error to [-pi, pi]
                while (heading_error > M_PI) heading_error -= 2 * M_PI;
                while (heading_error < -M_PI) heading_error += 2 * M_PI;

                yaw_cmd = heading_error * 0.3f;
                yaw_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, yaw_cmd));
            }

            // Control descent rate: aim for about -2 m/s
            if (s.vz > -1.5f) {
                thrust = 0.4f;  // Let gravity pull down
            } else if (s.vz < -3.0f) {
                thrust = 0.65f;  // Slow down descent
            } else {
                thrust = 0.5f;
            }
        }
        // Phase 2: Medium altitude stabilization
        else if (dz > 10.0f) {
            // Fine horizontal approach
            pitch_cmd = dx * 0.03f;
            yaw_cmd = -dy * 0.03f;

            pitch_cmd = max(-0.5f, min(0.5f, pitch_cmd));
            yaw_cmd = max(-0.5f, min(0.5f, yaw_cmd));

            // Gentle descent
            if (s.vz > -1.0f) {
                thrust = 0.5f;
            } else if (s.vz < -2.0f) {
                thrust = 0.6f;
            } else {
                thrust = 0.55f;
            }
        }
        // Phase 3: Final approach (landing)
        else {
            // Counteract horizontal velocity
            pitch_cmd = s.vx * 0.12f;
            yaw_cmd = -s.vy * 0.08f;

            // Very gentle descent - minimum thrust variation
            if (abs(s.vz) < 0.2f) {
                thrust = 0.5f;
            } else if (s.vz > -0.3f) {
                thrust = 0.48f;
            } else {
                thrust = 0.52f;
            }
        }

        // Clamp all commands to valid ranges
        thrust = max(0.0f, min(1.0f, thrust));
        pitch_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, pitch_cmd));
        yaw_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, yaw_cmd));
        roll_cmd = max(-1.0f, min(1.0f, roll_cmd));

        // Output control command with proper precision

             << thrust << " " << pitch_cmd << " " << yaw_cmd << " " << roll_cmd << "\n";
        cout.flush();

        auto now = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();

            this_thread::sleep_for(chrono::milliseconds(100 - ms_elapsed));
        }

}

    // Disable synchronization between C and C++ stdio for faster I/O

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**

Launch an interceptor spacecraft to collide with a dangerous asteroid before it reaches Earth. The AI must plan a trajectory through the solar system, accounting for the gravitational pull of the Sun and planets, to hit a moving target millions of kilometres away.

Harder subpasses give less warning time or tighter fuel budgets, potentially requiring planetary gravity assists (slingshots) to reach the asteroid. The AI must implement orbital mechanics and trajectory optimisation from scratch.

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.IO;

class AsteroidInterceptionSolver
{
    const double G = 6.67430e-11;
    const double AU = 1.496e11;
    const double EARTH_MASS = 5.972e24;
    const double SUN_MASS = 1.989e30;
    const double IMPACT_DISTANCE = 1000000; // 1000 km in meters

    struct Body
    {
        public string name;
        public double mass;
        public Vector3 pos;
        public Vector3 vel;
    }

    struct Burn
    {
        public double time;
        public Vector3 dv;
    }

    struct Vector3
    {
        public double x, y, z;

        public Vector3(double x = 0, double y = 0, double z = 0)
        {
            this.x = x;
            this.y = y;
            this.z = z;
        }

        public static Vector3 operator +(Vector3 a, Vector3 b) => new Vector3(a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y, a.z + b.z);
        public static Vector3 operator -(Vector3 a, Vector3 b) => new Vector3(a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y, a.z - b.z);
        public static Vector3 operator *(Vector3 a, double s) => new Vector3(a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s);
        public static Vector3 operator *(double s, Vector3 a) => new Vector3(a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s);

        public double Magnitude() => Math.Sqrt(x * x + y * y + z * z);
        public Vector3 Normalized() { var m = Magnitude(); return m > 1e-10 ? new Vector3(x / m, y / m, z / m) : new Vector3(); }
        public double Dot(Vector3 b) => x * b.x + y * b.y + z * b.z;
        public Vector3 Cross(Vector3 b) => new Vector3(y * b.z - z * b.y, z * b.x - x * b.z, x * b.y - y * b.x);
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        var input = Console.In.ReadToEnd();
        var tokens = input.Split(new[] { ' ', '\t', '\n', '\r' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        var idx = 0;

        int numBodies = int.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
        int warningDays = int.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
        double impactorMass = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
        double deltaVBudget = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
        double asteroidMass = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
        long t0UnixUtc = long.Parse(tokens[idx++]);

        var bodies = new List<Body>();
        Body earth = default;
        Body asteroid = default;

        for (int i = 0; i < numBodies; i++)
        {
            var body = new Body();
            body.name = tokens[idx++];
            body.mass = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
            body.pos = new Vector3(double.Parse(tokens[idx++]), double.Parse(tokens[idx++]), double.Parse(tokens[idx++]));
            body.vel = new Vector3(double.Parse(tokens[idx++]), double.Parse(tokens[idx++]), double.Parse(tokens[idx++]));

            bodies.Add(body);
            if (body.name == "Earth") earth = body;
            if (body.name == "Asteroid") asteroid = body;
        }

        string de421Path = idx < tokens.Length ? tokens[idx] : "";

        var burns = PlanTrajectory(bodies, earth, asteroid, warningDays, deltaVBudget, asteroidMass, impactorMass);

        Console.WriteLine(burns.Count);
        foreach (var burn in burns)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{burn.time} {burn.dv.x} {burn.dv.y} {burn.dv.z}");
        }
    }

    static List<Burn> PlanTrajectory(List<Body> bodies, Body earth, Body asteroid, int warningDays, double deltaVBudget, double asteroidMass, double impactorMass)
    {
        var burns = new List<Burn>();

        // Find collision time (when asteroid reaches Earth's position)
        double collisionTime = FindCollisionTime(earth, asteroid, warningDays);

        if (collisionTime <= 0 || collisionTime > warningDays)
        {
            // Asteroid not on collision course or won't collide in warning window
            return burns;
        }

        // Plan intercept
        double interceptTime = collisionTime * 0.95; // Intercept slightly before collision

        // Launch burn: accelerate from Earth toward asteroid
        var earthToAsteroid = asteroid.pos - earth.pos;
        double distance = earthToAsteroid.Magnitude();

        // Estimate required delta-v for Hohmann-like transfer
        double launchDv = EstimateLaunchDv(earth, asteroid, interceptTime);

        if (launchDv > deltaVBudget)
        {
            // Try direct ascent with all available delta-v
            launchDv = deltaVBudget * 0.6; // Reserve some for corrections and impact
        }

        // Launch immediately
        Vector3 launchDirection = earthToAsteroid.Normalized();
        var launchBurn = new Burn
        {
            time = 1.0,
            dv = launchDirection * launchDv
        };
        burns.Add(launchBurn);

        double usedDv = launchDv;
        double remainingDv = deltaVBudget - usedDv;

        // Mid-course corrections
        // Simple approach: one correction halfway to intercept
        if (remainingDv > 100 && interceptTime > 5)
        {
            double correctionTime = interceptTime * 0.5;
            Vector3 correctionDv = PlanCorrectionBurn(earth, asteroid, launchBurn, correctionTime, interceptTime);
            double correctionMag = correctionDv.Magnitude();

            if (correctionMag > 0 && correctionMag < remainingDv * 0.5)
            {
                var correctionBurn = new Burn { time = correctionTime, dv = correctionDv };
                burns.Add(correctionBurn);
                usedDv += correctionMag;
                remainingDv -= correctionMag;
            }
        }

        // Final impact burn
        // Calculate deflection needed based on kinetic impact
        double deflectionNeeded = CalculateDeflectionNeeded(earth, asteroid, asteroidMass, impactorMass);
        Vector3 impactDv = CalculateImpactBurn(asteroid, deflectionNeeded, remainingDv * 0.4);

        if (impactDv.Magnitude() > 0.1)
        {
            var impactBurn = new Burn
            {
                time = interceptTime,
                dv = impactDv
            };
            burns.Add(impactBurn);
        }

        return burns;
    }

    static double FindCollisionTime(Body earth, Body asteroid, int maxDays)
    {
        const double dt = 0.1; // 0.1 day steps
        Vector3 earthPos = earth.pos;
        Vector3 earthVel = earth.vel;
        Vector3 asteroidPos = asteroid.pos;
        Vector3 asteroidVel = asteroid.vel;

        for (double t = 0; t <= maxDays; t += dt)
        {
            earthPos = earthPos + earthVel * dt;
            asteroidPos = asteroidPos + asteroidVel * dt;

            var diff = asteroidPos - earthPos;
            double distance = diff.Magnitude();

            if (distance < AU * 0.1) // Within ~15 million km (rough collision zone)
            {
                if (t > 0.1) return t;
            }
        }

        return maxDays + 1; // Not on collision course
    }

    static double EstimateLaunchDv(Body earth, Body asteroid, double interceptTime)
    {
        var rel = asteroid.pos - earth.pos;
        double distance = rel.Magnitude();
        double timeToIntercept = interceptTime;

        // Simple estimate: need to cover distance in given time
        double requiredVel = distance / (timeToIntercept * 86400);
        double earthOrbitVel = earth.vel.Magnitude();

        return Math.Sqrt(requiredVel * requiredVel + earthOrbitVel * earthOrbitVel) - earthOrbitVel;
    }

    static Vector3 PlanCorrectionBurn(Body earth, Body asteroid, Burn previousBurn, double correctionTime, double targetTime)
    {
        // Estimate spacecraft position after launch
        Vector3 spacecraftPos = earth.pos;
        Vector3 spacecraftVel = earth.vel + previousBurn.dv;

        double dt = correctionTime;
        spacecraftPos = spacecraftPos + spacecraftVel * dt;

        // Target position
        Vector3 targetPos = asteroid.pos + asteroid.vel * dt;

        // Calculate correction needed
        var diff = targetPos - spacecraftPos;
        double distance = diff.Magnitude();

        if (distance < 1e9) return new Vector3(); // Already close enough

        // Apply simple correction
        double correctionMag = Math.Min(distance / (targetTime - correctionTime) / 86400, 500);
        return diff.Normalized() * correctionMag;
    }

    static double CalculateDeflectionNeeded(Body earth, Body asteroid, double asteroidMass, double impactorMass)
    {
        var relPos = asteroid.pos - earth.pos;
        var relVel = asteroid.vel - earth.vel;

        double distance = relPos.Magnitude();
        double relSpeed = relVel.Magnitude();

        // Needed velocity change to avoid collision (simplified)
        // Assume collision zone is ~100,000 km
        double collisionZone = 1e8;
        double deflectionNeeded = (collisionZone / distance) * relSpeed;

        // Momentum transfer efficiency
        double efficiency = impactorMass / asteroidMass;

        return deflectionNeeded / efficiency;
    }

    static Vector3 CalculateImpactBurn(Body asteroid, double deflectionNeeded, double maxDv)
    {
        // Direction roughly perpendicular to Earth impact trajectory
        Vector3 impactDir = new Vector3(1, 0.5, 0.2).Normalized();
        double magnitude = Math.Min(deflectionNeeded * 0.1, maxDv);
        return impactDir * magnitude;
    }
}

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.
